---
title: "LRP-RLI-LCF-001: Longitudinal correlated-domain-factor model (per-wave latent skill correlations)"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

::: {.callout-note}
Drafted by a LLM-based AI tool (Claude Code/Fable 5).

Substantially edited by a LLM-based AI tool (Codex/GPT-5).
:::

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

LRP-RLI-LCF-001 is the four-wave extension of the cross-sectional
[`corr_factor`](../lrp-rli-mm-001/) CFA. It estimates **correlated domain factors** —
vocabulary, code, and grammar — at each of the four timepoints and reports the
**per-wave latent skill correlation matrices** together with the conditional latent slopes
derived from them. It is a latent-measurement companion to the
concurrent regression family ([`lrp-rli-ca-001`](../lrp-rli-ca-001/), issue #312): the
regression family gives directed, covariate-conditional observed-score slopes; this
summarises a latent-domain correlation web while modelling indicator-specific residual
variation. Only the latent correlation matrix is symmetric; its conditional slopes are
directional and generally asymmetric. The two analyses have different estimands, so
neither must be larger. A reproducible 32-row report artefact aligns selected LCF
target-item translations with the matching #312 adjusted average marginal effects by
target, predictor and wave.

::: {.callout-important title="Measurement / triangulation, NOT causal"}
This is a **measurement** model. Every latent correlation and every slope derived from
it is a **descriptive association** (locked DAG, ID-2); the instrument improves the
measurement match, it does **not** identify effects. The randomised causal claims live
in the ITT/DiD suites and the mediation models, not here.
:::

::: {.callout-note title="Fragility at n ~ 54"}
A longitudinal latent model at n ~ 54 is fragile and prior-dependent — more so than the
single-wave `mm-001`, since it carries a trait correlation matrix and one state
correlation matrix per wave. The reporting-tier fit gives nominal, exploratory
estimates, not final scientific magnitudes. Prior sensitivity is required before
substantive interpretation. Wave-varying loadings or an AR across-wave structure should
be fitted only if checks indicate that the wave-invariant measurement specification or
compound symmetry misfits, then compared using per-child PSIS-LOO.
:::

## Model

**Factors.** Vocabulary {R, E, TR, TE}, code {L, B}, grammar {F, T} — taught vocabulary
folds into the vocabulary domain because all four indicators are administered and modelled at every wave; occasional missing cells are masked rather than dropping a child. Heavily floored P/N are excluded.

**Measurement (wave-invariant parameters, factor scores marginalised out).** For indicator $j$
of domain $d$ at wave $t$, on the pooled-standardised logit scale, with wave-invariant
loadings $\lambda_j$ and residual SDs $\sigma_j$:

$$
z_{ijt} = \lambda_j\, f_{i,d(j),t} + \varepsilon_{ijt}, \qquad
\varepsilon_{ijt} \sim \mathcal{N}(0, \sigma_j^2),
$$

with per-wave factor means carried by an exact zero-sum-over-waves $\mu_{d,t}$. Each
factor is unit-variance at every wave. The reported within-wave correlation is induced
by the trait/state structure below, not drawn directly from a separate LKJ prior.

**Across-wave (trait / state).** $f_{i,d,t} = \sqrt{\pi_d}\,\tau_{i,d} +
\sqrt{1-\pi_d}\,s_{i,d,t}$: a stable per-child trait $\tau_i \sim \mathcal{N}(0,
\Omega_\tau)$ (shared across waves) plus a wave-specific state $s_{i,t} \sim
\mathcal{N}(0, \Omega_{s,t})$ (independent across waves). The trait matrix
$\Omega_\tau$ and each state matrix $\Omega_{s,t}$ receive LKJ priors. For domains
$d$ and $e$, the reported within-wave correlation is

$$
\operatorname{Corr}(f_{d,t}, f_{e,t}) =
\sqrt{\pi_d\pi_e}\,\Omega_{\tau,de} +
\sqrt{(1-\pi_d)(1-\pi_e)}\,\Omega_{s,t,de}.
$$

This is PSD by construction and preserves unit factor variance. The stable trait term
couples the reported matrices across waves; for the same domain, the correlation
between any two waves equals its trait share $\pi_d$ under the compound-symmetry
starting assumption.

The Gaussian factor scores are **marginalised out** (the `mm-001` funnel fix): each
child's observed indicator cells are an $\mathcal{MVN}$ with covariance $\Sigma_z =
\Lambda \Sigma_f \Lambda' + \operatorname{diag}(\sigma^2)$ sliced to that child's
observed cells — no per-child latent RV, so no funnel. Missing cells are masked, not
dropped (one $\mathcal{MVN}$ per observed-cell pattern), so a child missing one wave
still contributes its other cells.

![Model diagram](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_results_long_corr_factor.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
